plays.title: The Barbel Memoir
plays.description: Büchner dissects a fish's nervous system and writes the French memoir that earns him a doctorate in exile.

plays.title: Correspondence
plays.description: A desperate web of ink and sealing wax carries a fugitive's survival across borders.

plays.title: Danton's Death
plays.description: A weary revolutionary is crushed by the relentless machinery of historical virtue.

plays.title: Hugo Translations
plays.description: A fugitive translates French dramas in feverish night work to finance his flight.

plays.title: Lenz
plays.description: A poet flees into the cold mountains of madness, searching for an anchor for his mind.

plays.title: Leonce and Lena
plays.description: Two bored royal children flee their crowns only to marry as wooden automatons.

plays.title: Lucretia Borgia
plays.description: A soldier unknowingly stabs his mother, the infamous duchess, to avenge his poisoned friends.

plays.title: Maria Tudor
plays.description: A queen sets her unfaithful lover a deadly trap of treason and execution.

plays.title: On Cranial Nerves
plays.description: Galvanic current twitches a dead fish to prove that life is a biological machine.

plays.title: The Philosophy Scripts
plays.description: A lecturer drafts lectures on Descartes and Spinoza for a chair he will never hold.

plays.title: Aretino (The Fireplace)
plays.description: A lost masterpiece of Renaissance satire rises as ash from the betrothed's fireplace.

plays.title: The Selecta
plays.description: A schoolboy writes three texts on death and defends Cato's suicide before his teachers.

plays.title: The Society for Human Rights
plays.description: A student secret society swears its oath to revolution before the gendarmes crush the alliance.

plays.title: The Strasbourg Emigration
plays.description: A fugitive finds asylum with his beloved in Strasbourg while debating exile with democrats.

plays.title: The Hessian Courier
plays.description: A revolutionary pamphlet uses bare tax statistics to ignite a peasant uprising.

plays.title: The Wanted Poster
plays.description: A student is arrested as a poet writes his work and flees before the wanted poster is issued.

plays.title: Weidig's Martyrdom
plays.description: A pastor is interrogated, keeps silent in the dungeon, and dies in his cell under unexplained circumstances.

plays.title: Woyzeck
plays.description: A simple soldier breaks under orders, hunger, and a knife.

plays.title: The Young Germany Censorship
plays.description: An editor fights for a drama before an imperial decree bans Young Germany.

plays.title: The Spiegelgasse (The Zurich Exile)
plays.description: A materialist doctor dissects the barbel's nerves as typhus claims his body.
